h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to correctly identify and state the components of the carbon tile as guided by the tutor's scaffolding.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
Upper mark, 6: atomic number.
Symbol box, C: symbol.
Name line, carbon: name.
Lower mark, 12.01: atomic mass.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully uses spatial position to recall the hidden labels, completing the new recall task introduced by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner pressure or breakdown that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or change route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present as there was no reversal trigger.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence:
Upper mark, 6: atomic number.
Symbol box, C: symbol.
Name line, carbon: name.
Lower mark, 12.01: atomic mass.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully uses spatial position to recall the hidden labels, completing the new recall task introduced by the tutor.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner pressure or breakdown that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or change route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner pressure or breakdown that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or change route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present as there was no reversal trigger.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply complete the list of forces using the established source-on-cart formula.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The two table cards need their force names first, or they look too much alike.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner enacts a new ordering criterion for the labels to resolve the visual ambiguity they encountered.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That separation stays. Use the card name, then the source: ___ on cart. Read the full set from the marks once.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's physical separation and introduces a new syntactic mechanism to resolve the naming ambiguity.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use the card name, then the source: ___ on cart.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, directly fitted to the learner's confusion, and provides a clear, actionable constraint that prevents the cards from looking alike.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The two table cards need their force names first, or they look too much alike.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner enacts a new ordering criterion for the labels to resolve the visual ambiguity they encountered.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: That separation stays. Use the card name, then the source: ___ on cart. Read the full set from the marks once.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's physical separation and introduces a new syntactic mechanism to resolve the naming ambiguity.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: That separation stays. Use the card name, then the source: ___ on cart. Read the full set from the marks once.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's physical separation and introduces a new syntactic mechanism to resolve the naming ambiguity.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Use the card name, then the source: ___ on cart.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, directly fitted to the learner's confusion, and provides a clear, actionable constraint that prevents the cards from looking alike.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 still pulls toward speed for me
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was initially focused on testing the 3/4 time signature to see if the bottom number 4 dictated the speed of the measure.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note spaces, so the remaining single eighth-note card fills the gap. Next check: a dotted quarter should cover all three eighth-note units.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new beat-unit concept to calculate a rhythmic gap and proactively proposes a valid next test using a dotted quarter note.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's prior speed misconception with a new representation that forces the learner to calculate unit coverage rather than speed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, directly targets the learner's speed misconception by changing the denominator to 8, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for counting unit coverage.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor immediately adopts the learner's proposed hypothetical from the pivot turn and turns it into a concrete physical task with the eighth-note card.
